historical_center: persisted runtime sessions and API entry are first-class
current_branch_center: runtime state, reporting, scheduler, and service harnesses are distributed across kernel surfaces
delta_judgment: current branch lacks one first-class runtime/session center
reintegration_target: restore separate runtime session authority protocol
risk_if_misread: mistaking truthful runtime witnesses for a full runtime organism

historical_center: sessions persist as durable service objects with dispatch history
current_branch_center: scope-bound manifest and automation records exist, but session identity is not the center
delta_judgment: current branch is stronger on state truth than session identity
reintegration_target: restore explicit runtime session identity semantics
risk_if_misread: treating work-unit or manifest scope as equivalent to runtime session continuity

historical_center: per-session queues support sequential, timed, dependency-aware behavior
current_branch_center: dispatch posture and scheduling exist, but no first-class session queue law is active
delta_judgment: historical center is stronger on queue lifecycle detail
reintegration_target: restore session queue and dispatch lifecycle protocol
risk_if_misread: letting queue semantics hide inside ad hoc service code or scheduler wording

historical_center: scheduler tick drains runnable queued work inside runtime service
current_branch_center: lawful scheduler compiles future work and dispatch reconciliation ties schedules to assignments
delta_judgment: current branch scheduler is broader but not session-runtime specific
reintegration_target: keep project scheduler distinct from session queue control
risk_if_misread: overloading the project scheduler with API runtime responsibilities

historical_center: HTTP runtime endpoints create, inspect, and drive sessions directly
current_branch_center: operator CLI, supervised daemon service, and external bridge expose service access without first-class API runtime center
delta_judgment: current branch is stronger on supervision than API-runtime completeness
reintegration_target: restore explicit API runtime entry protocol
risk_if_misread: assuming CLI or daemon surfaces already equal a full API runtime

historical_center: service records and queue artifacts are operationally useful but less explicit about truth classes
current_branch_center: route-state, automation-state, and runtime reporting keep kernel truth and service witness separate
reintegration_target: preserve runtime-state binding/query/reporting as bounded substrate
risk_if_misread: losing truth separation during runtime restoration

historical_center: session queue dispatch carries provider and engine policy at runtime edge
current_branch_center: governed dispatch, execution return, and review thresholds are stronger in kernel law
reintegration_target: link restored session queueing to existing governed dispatch path
risk_if_misread: restoring sessions in a way that bypasses current dispatch/review discipline

historical_center: runtime shell trends service-native and automation-forward
current_branch_center: daemon service and external bridges are explicitly supervised and bounded
delta_judgment: current branch is safer on anti-autonomy posture
reintegration_target: preserve supervised service law during restoration
risk_if_misread: reviving hidden unattended runtime claims under session language
